Programmable Medicine

Cancer therapy has two problems — one target can't solve both

Pushing one target harder trades one problem for the other → we need a logic rule (OR · AND · NOT), not a single target.

2
The shift — program the medicine

Combine targets with logic: OR for coverage, AND / NOT for specificity

Instead of one target = one drug, specify a rule:

IF (antigen A OR antigen B)
  AND (tumor protease active)
  NOT (normal-tissue marker)
THEN → act
  • OR two antigens → reach more cancer cells (coverage ↑)
  • AND a tumor-environment permit & NOT a normal-tissue marker → fire in tumor only (specificity ↑)

Medicines are programmable:

CAR-T is the flagship — engineered T-cells with synthetic receptors. Masked / logic-gated CAR-T already implement AND/OR conditions.

Molecules too — Probody / Pro-antibody ADCs (CX-072, CX-2029) are protease-activated, antigen-directed. Same logic rule, built into a molecule.

The goal of this pilot is to find the logic gate for treating TNBC — so we can program the medicine from the result.

3
How we built it — data & functions

From candidate pools to scored logic gates

Data: SURFY surfaceome · TCGA-BRCA Basal-like (TNBC proxy, n=171) · GTEx 54 normal tissues · CELLxGENE single-cell TNBC · UCSC Xena TOIL (GTEx+TCGA on one scale). Key rule: specificity and coverage are scored on separate axes and recombined only at the finished-logic level.

4
Result 1 — coverage

OR-ing two antigens widens tumor-cell coverage

Single EPCAM reaches 47% of malignant cells (single-cell detection floor). EPCAM OR VTCN1 → 51%+ — but OR alone leaves normal-tissue leak (kidney 172 · pancreas 67 · lung 64 TPM). That is what the AND condition fixes.

5
Result 2 — specificity via gating

A protease AND-gate silences the normal-tissue leak

Surface antigens (top) leak into kidney, lung, pancreas. The gate protease MMP11 is near-zero there (kidney 1 · pancreas 0 · lung 1) but active in TNBC (52) — so (antigen) AND (MMP11) fires in tumor only. Housekeeping proteases (ADAM10, active in 21 normal tissues) were excluded.

6
Result 3 — recommendation

Logic gates reach high coverage and high safety

Every dot is a candidate logic. Gates (green) occupy the high-coverage, high-safety corner that no single antigen reaches.

TOP RECOMMENDATION
(EPCAM OR VTCN1)
AND MMP11
coverage 51% · safety 0.96 · final 0.83
tie-compatible proteases: ADAM12, FAP

Same rule → two modalities: masked CAR-T (protease unmasks, antigen kills — antigen is the backup) or Pro-antibody ADC (protease-cleaved release — needs the strictest protease, MMP11).

7
The bigger map

This pilot filled 2 cells of a 3 × 5 input space

Every targetable signal is a (WHERE × WHAT) cell. We used cell-surface protein (address) + extracellular protease (permit). The other cells — metabolites, pH/hypoxia, intracellular RNA, neoantigens — extend the same logic-gating framework.

8
Where this goes

Tokenize the cell. Compile the medicine.

Represent any cell as its DNA · RNA · surface · environment / state — a set of readable tokens.

cell = { DNA: [mutations…], RNA: [expression…],
       surface: [antigens…], environment: [proteases, pH, metabolites…] }

→ recommend( targets, logic )   →   design( medicine )

And the tokens are predictable: just as cell × perturbation models predict a response, a cell's DNA (genotype) × STATE (where it sits — tissue, niche, TME) should predict its full RNA / surface / environment profile.

predict( DNA, state ) → { RNA, surface, environment tokens }
→ recommend( targets, logic ) → design( medicine )

Given the tokens, the system proposes what to target and which logic to build — then it hands off to the module that programs the medicine to run that logic gate.

TNBC was one query. The framework is general.
